div1.entryFree:
Tendance, attendance; 1) waiting on, attention, care: subdues and properties to his love and t. all sorts of hearts, Tim. I, 1, 57. she purposed, by watching, weeping, t., kissing, to o'ercome you with her show, Cymb. V, 5, 53. With to: nature does require her times of preservation, which perforce I, her frail son, . . . must give my t. to, H8 III, 2, 149.
2) persons attending: his lobbies fill with t. Tim. I, 1, 80.
